cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why it was one of the author's happiest times. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to go back to the passage. The first sentence says, "It was one of the happiest times of my life. I was 29 and had just received my bachelor's degree, graduating with honors despite working two jobs and being a wife and mother." So the main event here is receiving her bachelor's degree with honors. The other things mentioned are working two jobs and being a wife and mother, but the key point is the graduation. Looking at the options: A says she became a good wife and got two jobs. But the passage mentions her working two jobs as a challenge she overcame while being a wife and mother, not that becoming a good wife was the happy time. The happy time is the graduation. B says she had just received her bachelor's degree. This matches the first sentence where the happiest time is when she received her degree with honors. C says she was a teacher at Ashland University. The passage mentions Ashland University as the place where she got her diploma, not that she was a teacher there.
